operating_state: Total revenue increased 22% to $164.5 billion driven by advertising revenue growth across Family of Apps, with Reality Labs contributing from consumer hardware sales. Ad impressions delivered increased 11% while average price per ad rose 10%, reflecting strong advertiser demand and improved targeting capabilities. Daily active people grew 5% to 3.35 billion with worldwide ARPP increasing 15%, demonstrating broad-based strength across geographic regions including notable China revenue expansion.
cost_structure: Income from operations expanded 48% with operating margin improving to 42% from 35% in the prior year. Research and development expenses increased driven by headcount growth in engineering functions supporting AI initiatives and Reality Labs investments. Cost of revenue increased primarily from higher data center and technical infrastructure depreciation expense, while Reality Labs operating losses reduced consolidated operating profit as the segment scales metaverse development.
material_events: Board initiated quarterly dividend program at $0.50 per share beginning February 2024 while completing share repurchases. Board expanded to thirteen members with election of Dana White, John Elkann, and Charles Songhurst as independent directors effective December 30, 2024. European Commission issued competition fine for Facebook Marketplace tying practices, currently under appeal.
macro_exposures: European regulatory environment intensified with formal Digital Services Act proceedings regarding content moderation requirements and GDPR fine from Irish Data Protection Commission under appeal. Apple iOS changes continue negatively impacting ad targeting capabilities while multiple U.S. states enacted legislation restricting services to minors with various enforcement injunctions creating compliance uncertainty. Foreign currency translation losses increased reflecting strengthening dollar impacts on international operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures of $60-65 billion in 2025 to support core business and generative AI infrastructure investments, with Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase beyond 2024 levels. Effective tax rate projected at 12-15% for 2025 assuming current stock price levels, while server and network asset useful life extension will reduce depreciation expense. Continued quarterly dividend payments subject to board discretion with remaining share repurchase authorization.
uncertainty_profile: FTC administrative proceeding outcome could impose severe operational restrictions including prohibition on minor data usage and mandatory business practice modifications, with antitrust trial scheduled April 2025. Transfer pricing disputes with IRS continue in Tax Court with potential federal tax liability plus interest and penalties. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings including consolidated youth-related litigation and European privacy investigations present substantial compliance costs with inherently unpredictable outcomes.
